sentence: O céu nom está claro hoje.
ipa: ˈo ˈθɛu ˈnom eˈsta ˈklaɾo ˈoʃe
gloss_en: The sky is not clear today.
notes: Reintegrationist spelling ⟨hoje⟩ with etymological ⟨j⟩ [ʃ] (Galician has no /ʒ/, so ⟨j⟩ is voiceless); distinción ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'céu'; open-mid [ɛ]. Word-final ⟨-m⟩ 'nom' surfaces [m] here — see the PR velar-nasal lead — per regueira1996.

sentence: Estes çapatos custam quarenta euros.
ipa: ˈestes θaˈpatos ˈkustam kaˈɾenta ˈewɾos
gloss_en: These shoes cost forty euros.
notes: ⟨ç⟩ restored for /θ/ 'çapatos' [θapatos] — the reintegrationist grapheme the RAG norm lacks; phonology identical to standard Galician per regueira1996.

sentence: A junta é manhá às tres.
ipa: ˈa ˈʃunta ˈɛ maˈɲa ˈs ˈtɾes
gloss_en: The gathering is tomorrow at three.
notes: Etymological ⟨j⟩ [ʃ] 'junta'; palatal nasal ⟨nh⟩ [ɲ] 'manhá'; open-mid [ɛ] 'é' per regueira1996.

sentence: Meu irmao vive perto da vila.
ipa: ˈmew ˈiɾmao ˈbibe ˈpeɾto ˈda ˈbila
gloss_en: My brother lives near the town.
notes: Reintegrationist ⟨irmao⟩; falling diphthong 'meu' [ew]; betacism ⟨v⟩=[b] 'vive' per regueira1996.

sentence: Vai de fronte e torce pola primeira rua.
ipa: ˈbaj ˈde ˈfɾonte ˈe ˈtoɾθe ˈpola pɾiˈmejɾa ˈrwa
gloss_en: Go straight ahead and turn down the first street.
notes: No Romance diphthongisation 'fronte'/'torce'; word-initial trill [r] 'rua'; distinción 'torce' [θ] per regueira1996.

sentence: A caldeirada daquel sítio estava moi boa.
ipa: ˈa kaldeˈjɾaða daˈkel ˈsitjo eˈstaba ˈmoj ˈboa
gloss_en: The fish stew at that place was very good.
notes: Same phonology as the RAG norm under reintegrationist spelling; regional 'caldeirada'; hiatus sandhi per regueira1996.

sentence: Onde está a chave do coche?
ipa: ˈonde eˈsta ˈa ˈtʃabe ˈdo ˈkotʃe
gloss_en: Where is the car key?
notes: Affricate ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'chave'/'coche'; hiatus 'está a' per regueira1996.

sentence: Quantas pessoas colhem cerejas?
ipa: ˈkantas ˈpesoas ˈkoʎem θeˈɾeʃas
gloss_en: How many people are picking cherries?
notes: Palatal lateral ⟨lh⟩ [ʎ] 'colhem' (the reintegrationist grapheme for /ʎ/, RAG ⟨ll⟩); distinción ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'cerejas' per regueira1996.

sentence: Se eu podesse, mercaria aquela casa grande.
ipa: ˈse ˈew poˈðese meˈɾkaɾja aˈkela ˈkasa ˈɡɾande
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy that big house.
notes: Regional 'mercar'; betacism ⟨v⟩=[b]; hiatus 'se eu' per regueira1996.

sentence: Onte merquei cinco quilos de cerejas na praça.
ipa: ˈonte ˈmeɾkej ˈθinko ˈkilos ˈde θeˈɾeʃas ˈna ˈpɾaθa
gloss_en: Yesterday I bought five kilos of cherries in the square.
notes: Distinción ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'cinco'/'cerejas' with ⟨ç⟩ restored 'praça' [pɾaθa]; etymological ⟨j⟩ [ʃ] 'cerejas' per regueira1996.

sentence: O ano que vem hei viajar a Compostela.
ipa: ˈo ˈano ˈke ˈbem ˈej bjaˈʃaɾ ˈa kompoˈstela
gloss_en: Next year I will travel to Compostela.
notes: Etymological ⟨j⟩ [ʃ] 'viajar'; word-final ⟨-m⟩ 'vem' surfaces [m] (velar-nasal gap, see PR lead) per regueira1996.

sentence: Chama-me quando chegues á casa.
ipa: ˈtʃama ˈme ˈkando ˈtʃeɡes ˈa ˈkasa
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: Affricate ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'chama'/'chegues'; enclitic 'chama-me' per regueira1996.

sentence: Boas tardes, como che vai a vida?
ipa: ˈboas ˈtaɾdes ˈkomo ˈtʃe ˈbaj ˈa ˈbiða
gloss_en: Good afternoon, how is life treating you?
notes: Clitic ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'che'; hiatus 'vai a' per regueira1996.

sentence: Mais vale passaro na mao que cento voando.
ipa: ˈmajs ˈbale paˈsaɾo ˈna ˈmao ˈke ˈθento ˈboando
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: ⟨ss⟩ [s] 'passaro' and distinción ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'cento'; reintegrationist ⟨mao⟩ per regueira1996.

sentence: O cam gordo corre pola ribeira do rio.
ipa: ˈo ˈkam ˈɡoɾdo ˈkore ˈpola riˈβejɾa ˈdo ˈrjo
gloss_en: The fat dog runs along the riverbank.
notes: Word-initial and ⟨rr⟩ trill [r] 'corre'/'rio'; reintegrationist ⟨cam⟩ with final ⟨-m⟩ [m] (velar-nasal gap) per regueira1996.

sentence: A mulher velha colhe a agulha amarela.
ipa: ˈa muˈʎeɾ ˈbeʎa ˈkoʎe ˈa ˈaɡʎa amaˈɾela
gloss_en: The old woman picks up the yellow needle.
notes: Palatal lateral ⟨lh⟩ [ʎ] 'mulher'/'velha'/'colhe'/'agulha' — the reintegrationist digraph for /ʎ/ per regueira1996.

sentence: O café da esquina é moi forte.
ipa: ˈo kaˈfɛ ˈda eˈskina ˈɛ ˈmoj ˈfoɾte
gloss_en: The coffee from the corner is very strong.
notes: Open-mid [ɛ] 'café'/'é'; hiatus 'esquina é' per regueira1996.

sentence: A vinha da montanha dá bom vinho.
ipa: ˈa ˈbiɲa ˈda moˈntaɲa ˈda ˈbom ˈbiɲo
gloss_en: The vineyard on the mountain gives good wine.
notes: Palatal nasal ⟨nh⟩ [ɲ] 'vinha'/'montanha'/'vinho'; ⟨bom⟩ final ⟨-m⟩ [m] per regueira1996.

sentence: Umha das mulheres colheu algumha flor.
ipa: ˈuma ˈdas muˈʎeɾes ˈkoʎew ˈalɡma ˈfloɾ
gloss_en: One of the women picked some flower.
notes: Palatal lateral ⟨lh⟩ [ʎ] 'mulheres'/'colheu'; reintegrationist ⟨umha⟩/⟨algumha⟩ [uma]/[alɡma] per regueira1996.

sentence: A praça da catedral tem doce colunas de pedra.
ipa: ˈa ˈpɾaθa ˈda kateˈdɾal ˈtem ˈdoθe koˈlunas ˈde ˈpedɾa
gloss_en: The cathedral square has twelve columns of stone.
notes: ⟨ç⟩ [θ] 'praça' and ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'doce'; ⟨tem⟩ final ⟨-m⟩ [m] (velar-nasal gap, see PR lead) per regueira1996.
